thinking_styles.description: Monolithic single-mind framing — the user reasons end-to-end as one unit, no internal decomposition surfaced.
Generate-then-merge framing — the user produces multiple independent candidates and combines them at the end.
Decompose-and-delegate framing — the user splits the task into sub-parts and asks for each to be handled, then integrated.
Compare-and-converge framing — the user weighs multiple perspectives or trade-offs and asks for a consensus answer.
Decompose-with-coordination framing — the user splits sub-tasks but explicitly asks the parts to talk to each other, not only via a central planner.
Sequential pipeline — first agent drafts, second refines, third finalizes; each builds on previous.

queries.independent:
Produce 3 fully independent end-to-end itineraries (no shared accommodation, transit, or restaurant choices between them), each shaped by a distinctly different priority — one cost-first, one comfort-first, one sights-first. Then pick the single strongest one as the final plan.

Please create a travel plan for me where I'll be departing from Washington and heading to Myrtle Beach for a 3-day trip from March 13th to March 15th, 2022. Can you help me keep this journey within a budget of $1,400?
queries.centralized:
Decompose the trip into sub-parts — (1) inter-city transit, (2) lodging, (3) meals and attractions — assign each sub-part to a specialist, then integrate their outputs into one unified final plan.

Please create a travel plan for me where I'll be departing from Washington and heading to Myrtle Beach for a 3-day trip from March 13th to March 15th, 2022. Can you help me keep this journey within a budget of $1,400?
queries.decentralized:
Evaluate the trip from multiple parallel angles — the cheapest configuration, the most schedule-feasible configuration, and the most rule-safe configuration — debate the trade-offs, and converge on the configuration that holds up best across all angles.

Please create a travel plan for me where I'll be departing from Washington and heading to Myrtle Beach for a 3-day trip from March 13th to March 15th, 2022. Can you help me keep this journey within a budget of $1,400?
queries.hybrid:
Decompose the trip into sub-parts (transit, lodging, meals and attractions) AND have the sub-part workers coordinate directly with each other for cross-dependent constraints (flight times ↔ hotel check-in windows; accommodation rules ↔ meal venues; daily budget split across categories). Do not route every decision through a single coordinator.

Please create a travel plan for me where I'll be departing from Washington and heading to Myrtle Beach for a 3-day trip from March 13th to March 15th, 2022. Can you help me keep this journey within a budget of $1,400?
queries.chain:
Build the trip plan through a sequential pipeline — (1) the first agent drafts an initial itinerary with rough candidates, (2) the second agent reviews and tightens constraints (budget, room rules, cuisine, no-duplication), (3) the third agent finalizes the plan in the required output format. Each agent builds on the previous agent's output without parallel work or peer cross-talk.
